name: bioregions-2023
title: Bioregions 2023
description: One Earth's Bioregions 2023 framework — 185 discrete bioregions organized within 52 subrealms, 14 realms, and 8 major biogeographical realms. A novel biogeographical classification of Earth's terrestrial surface, building on RESOLVE Ecoregions 2017 (844 ecoregions) and offering a nature-based alternative to political geography for conservation planning.

sources.name: Dinerstein et al. 2017
sources.title: An Ecoregion-Based Approach to Protecting Half the Terrestrial Realm, BioScience 2017 (RESOLVE Ecoregions 2017 — underlying framework)

resources.name: bioregions
resources.description: All 185 One Earth bioregions with their classification within the biogeographical hierarchy: biogeographic realm (8), One Earth realm (14), and subrealm (52). Each bioregion is identified by a two-letter realm code plus a number (e.g. NA1, AT12, PA53).

fields.name: code
fields.description: Bioregion identifier: two-letter biogeographic realm prefix (NA, NT, PA, AT, IM, AU, OC, AN) followed by a number (e.g. NA1, AT12, PA53)

fields.name: name
fields.description: Full name of the bioregion (e.g. 'Greater Eurasian Tundra', 'Amazon River Estuary')

fields.name: biogeographic_realm
fields.description: One of the eight traditional biogeographic realms: Nearctic, Neotropical, Palearctic, Afrotropical, Indomalayan, Australasian, Oceanian, Antarctic

fields.name: realm
fields.description: One Earth realm — 14 intermediate geographic divisions. The Nearctic splits into Subarctic America and Northern America; Neotropical into Central America and Southern America; Palearctic into Subarctic Eurasia, Western Eurasia, Central Eurasia, Eastern Eurasia, and Southern Eurasia. The remaining five biogeographic realms each map to a single One Earth realm.

fields.name: subrealm
fields.description: One Earth subrealm — 52 named groupings of ecologically related bioregions within each realm (e.g. 'Canadian Boreal Forests', 'Mediterranean', 'Amazonia')
